terms.definition: Define behavior, inputs, outputs, and error contracts in shared data before implementing language-specific runtime code.
terms.why_it_matters: Enables 1:1 behavior parity across wrappers and reduces divergence.
terms.anti_pattern: Hand-maintaining equivalent behavior in each language without a shared contract.

terms.definition: A function performs one cohesive action with a clear API contract.
terms.why_it_matters: Improves composability, testability, and benchmarking accuracy.
terms.anti_pattern: One function combining unrelated side effects and transformations.

terms.definition: A thin language-specific adapter that maps canonical contract fields to runtime calls and returns.
terms.why_it_matters: Allows language-native ergonomics while preserving shared semantics.
terms.anti_pattern: Embedding domain logic directly into each wrapper implementation.

terms.definition: Producing language-specific wrappers from shared contracts and symbol registries.
terms.why_it_matters: Reduces duplicated code and naming drift.
terms.anti_pattern: Copy-pasting wrappers and manually renaming symbols per language.

terms.definition: A structured architecture package combining model views, narrative constraints, and decision records.
terms.why_it_matters: Keeps architecture intent explicit and auditable as code evolves.
terms.anti_pattern: Single unstructured architecture note with no decision history.

terms.definition: Architecture views across system, container, component, and code levels.
terms.why_it_matters: Supports communication at multiple abstraction levels.
terms.anti_pattern: Only one diagram that mixes all concerns.

terms.definition: Architecture Decision Record documenting context, decision, and consequences.
terms.why_it_matters: Prevents repeated debate and preserves rationale.
terms.anti_pattern: Major design changes with no recorded rationale.

terms.definition: Shared behavior-based naming rules independent of language syntax style.
terms.why_it_matters: Allows consistent identifiers in catalogs while supporting local idioms.
terms.anti_pattern: Directly forcing one language style across all languages.

terms.definition: Mismatch between source contracts and generated/runtime implementations.
terms.why_it_matters: Causes subtle bugs, parity breaks, and invalid benchmarks.
terms.anti_pattern: Changing runtime behavior without revalidating source contracts.

terms.definition: Automated quality check that must pass before merge/release.
terms.why_it_matters: Makes standards enforceable rather than advisory.
terms.anti_pattern: Standards documented but not validated in CI.

terms.definition: External benchmark such as ASVS used to assess software controls.
terms.why_it_matters: Anchors internal checks to industry expectations.
terms.anti_pattern: Security checks based solely on ad-hoc team preference.

terms.definition: Confidence that source, builds, and artifacts are authentic and untampered.
terms.why_it_matters: Reduces risk of malicious or unverifiable build artifacts.
terms.anti_pattern: Releasing binaries without provenance or reproducibility controls.
